text: Large language models (LLMs) are computer programs that understand and generate human-like text. They work by learning patterns from huge amounts of written data, such as books, websites, and articles. During training, the model analyses billions of sentences to predict the next word in a sequence, gradually mastering grammar and facts. Users interact with LLMs through prompts—carefully written questions or instructions. Prompt engineering, or crafting effective prompts, helps get better results. LLMs can write essays, answer questions, and translate languages, but they have limits. They may produce incorrect or biased information and cannot truly understand meaning or emotions. So, while powerful, they should be used with caution.
